name: Arenoso Reservoir Loop from Montanejos
name_original: Rodeo al Embalse de Arenoso — Circular desde Montanejos
description: This difficult 20.5-mile mountain biking route from Montanejos offers challenging terrain and stunning views of the Embalse de Arenoso.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Montanejos offers a diverse range of trails within the Mijares Valley, characterized by lush green mountains, dramatic ravines, and the Mijares River carving through gorges. The region features varied terrain, including forest tracks, natural paths, and technical sections, providing options for different skill levels. Limestone walls and panoramic viewpoints are common features along the routes. Best mountain bike trails around Montanejos The most popular mountain bike trail is Arenoso Reservoir Loop from Montanejos, a 20.5 miles (33.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and views of the Arenós Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Alto Mijares — Circular desde Montanejos hasta el Morrón de Campos, a moderate 17.2 miles (27.7 km) path. This route explores the Alto Mijares region with varied terrain and notable elevation changes. Local mountain bikers also love the El Salto de la Novia — Circular desde Montanejos, a 33.7 miles (54.3 km) trail leading through the dramatic landscape of the Mijares Valley, often completed in about 5 hours 29 minutes. Mountain biking around Montanejos is defined by lush green mountains, dramatic ravines, and the scenic Mijares River. The network offers options for both challenging rides and more accessible paths. The routes in Montanejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from around 15 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Montanejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Montanejos area?
A: Montanejos offers a comprehensive network of mountain bike trails, with over 26 routes documented on komoot. The region is known for its extensive trail system, providing over 266 km of routes in total, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos is suitable for mountain biking year-round, thanks to its Mediterranean climate. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Montanejos?
A: While Montanejos is known for its challenging terrain, there are options for less experienced riders. The region features a mix of forest tracks and natural paths. Currently, komoot lists 7 moderate routes, which can be a good starting point for those with some experience, though truly easy routes for absolute beginners or young families are less common among the listed trails.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on Montanejos mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain around Montanejos is diverse, featuring lush green mountains, dramatic ravines, and the Mijares River carving through gorges. You'll encounter forest tracks, natural paths, and technical sections. Many routes involve significant elevation changes; for example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931163991/arenoso-reservoir-loop-from-montanejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenoso Reservoir Loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt; includes over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos trails offer stunning scenery. You can ride past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños - Montanejos Lake&lt;/a&gt;, known for its natural hot springs. Other notable features include the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5646525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrechos de Chillapájaros&lt;/a&gt; gorge, the Arenós Reservoir, and various panoramic viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3484565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de Piedrahíta Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Montanejos?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Montanejos are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931163991/arenoso-reservoir-loop-from-montanejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenoso Reservoir Loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931150392/el-alto-mijares-circular-desde-montanejos-hasta-el-morron-de-campos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Alto Mijares — Circular desde Montanejos hasta el Morrón de Campos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What facilities are available for mountain bikers in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos is well-equipped for cyclists. You can find services such as bike rentals, washing areas, self-workshops, guarded parking, and showers. The Asociación Btt Montanejos also provides information and support for mountain biking in the area.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Montanejos is a popular destination for experienced mountain bikers, with 19 of the 26 komoot routes classified as 'difficult'. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931182238/el-salto-de-la-novia-circular-desde-montanejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salto de la Novia — Circular desde Montanejos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931172272/monte-la-copa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte La Copa Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and technical sections, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass by swimming spots or water features?
A: Yes, the Mijares River is a central feature of the region, and many routes offer views or access to water. The famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños - Montanejos Lake&lt;/a&gt;, with its constant 25°C water, is a major attraction and can be incorporated into or visited after your ride. The Arenós Reservoir also provides serene waters.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Montanejos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Montanejos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from around 15 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, stunning natural beauty, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Montanejos?
A: Yes, Montanejos provides facilities for visiting cyclists, including guarded parking options. This makes it convenient to bring your bike and explore the extensive trail network.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer exceptional views of the Arenós Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931163991/arenoso-reservoir-loop-from-montanejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenoso Reservoir Loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer significant elevation gain and breathtaking views of the Arenós Reservoir. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enduroland MTB – View of Arenoso Reservoir loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt;, also provides excellent vistas of the reservoir.
tips: The Arenoso reservoir collects the waters of the Mijares river and creates an impressive landscape between the mountains and rocky slopes in which Puebla de Arenoso and Montanejos are located.
From the top of this mountainous rock in the end of Puebla de Arenoso, we have beautiful views and the Arenoso reservoir as the protagonist of the panorama.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Rodeo al Embalse de Arenoso mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult for mountain biking. It requires very good fitness and significant experience with technical terrain. Be prepared for sections that may require pushing your bike, with some parts reaching S3-S6 technical difficulty. One section, particularly the final 3 kilometers, has been described as 'alpinism impossible to travel with the bike' due to ravines, indicating its extreme nature.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Montanejos for this trail includes both fast sections and highly technical parts. You'll encounter roots, rocks, and descents that demand maximum skill. Given its high difficulty, expect very challenging paths, likely comprising rocky, uneven, and potentially steep sections, especially towards the end.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the stunning View of Arenoso Reservoir and the Viewpoint Over Arenoso Reservoir. The Embalse de Arenoso itself is renowned for its natural beauty, with crystal-clear turquoise waters set amidst mountains and lush forests. You'll find strategically placed viewpoints offering spectacular panoramic vistas of the reservoir and the Muela de Mijares.

faqs.question: Is the Rodeo al Embalse de Arenoso trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific restrictions for dogs on this particular mountain bike trail are not detailed, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash in natural parks and protected areas. Given the trail's extreme difficulty and technical sections, it might not be suitable for all dogs, especially those not accustomed to challenging mountain terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during many seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking in this region. Summers can be very hot, making the strenuous sections even more demanding. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Montanejos?
faqs.answer: Montanejos serves as an excellent hub for cyclists. You can typically find parking within the town of Montanejos itself, which is the starting point for this circular route. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors. For detailed information on parking options, you might consult local tourism resources like visitmontanejos.com.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Rodeo al Embalse de Arenoso trail. It is generally open to the public for recreational use. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing the Rodeo al Embalse de Arenoso — Circular desde Montanejos trail is approximately 3 hours and 36 minutes of moving time. However, due to its difficult rating and technical sections, experienced riders should allow for more time, especially if they plan to stop at viewpoints or navigate challenging parts carefully.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that align with trails such as La Conquista de Valencia, Camino del Cid (La Conquista de Valencia E06), Pista Almorocho, Camí de las Vinyas, and Senda Monzona - Puebla Arenoso, among others. These intersections can offer options for extending or modifying your ride if you're familiar with the area.

faqs.question: Are there any sections of the trail that are particularly difficult or require extra caution?
faqs.answer: Yes, riders should be aware that the final 3 kilometers of the trail are described as extremely challenging, with sections that resemble 'alpinism' and are considered impossible to travel with a bike due to ravines. This part demands maximum caution and may require dismounting and pushing your bike. Plan accordingly for this highly technical finish.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to Montanejos for accessing the trail?
faqs.answer: Montanejos is a popular destination, and while specific public transport details for reaching the trailhead are not provided, it is generally accessible by bus from larger nearby towns. It's recommended to check current bus schedules and routes to Montanejos if you plan to use public transport.
